page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is configured with 15 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. It has a dedicated CPU allocation and lacks local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests rank in the bottom 25%, with scores of 1107.78 and 2904.55 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 161.64 GB/sec for cached small blocks and drop to 25.29 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds fall into the bottom 10% of tested hardware.

From a workload perspective, the n1-standard-4 is suited for entry-level general-purpose applications, basic web serving, and lightweight database operations. Its static web serving throughput is 2.46 GB/sec, and it handles 2.68 million Redis SET operations per second. It is not designed for heavy computational tasks, large-scale LLM inference, or memory-intensive databases, but offers a balanced resource profile for legacy workloads or development environments.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 15 GB of RAM, powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built with 2 physical cores and hyper-threading, this instance provides a dedicated CPU allocation but includes no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark evaluations place its performance in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU and Passmark memory scores falling into the bottom 25%. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 2.46 GB/sec and processes 2.68 million Redis SET operations per second. Given these performance characteristics, the n1-standard-4 is best suited for lightweight workloads, development environments, basic web serving, and small-scale database operations where high-performance compute is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It is designed for lightweight workloads, basic web serving, and development.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-4 is a general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs and 15 GB RAM, suited for lightweight workloads and development.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon processors for lightweight workloads, development environments, and basic web serving.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 15 GB of RAM on an x86_64 Intel Xeon architecture
Dedicated CPU allocation with 2 physical cores and hyper-threading
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suitable for lightweight web serving, development, and basic databases
Lower-tier benchmark performance profile for cost-conscious workloads
